questions.text: What is the first step of the scientific method?
A scientist makes a guess about what might happen. This is called a:
What does a scientist do to test their prediction?
In the story, where did Mai find the open flowers?
options: In the shade under the mango tree
In the sunny part of the garden
questions.correctAnswer: In the sunny part of the garden

questions.text: What did Mai conclude about flowers?
options: Flowers open when they are in sunlight
questions.correctAnswer: Flowers open when they are in sunlight

questions.text: What is the last step of the scientific method?
Which are steps in the scientific method? (Select all that apply)
What senses do scientists use to observe? (Select all that apply)
In the reading passage, what did Mai do as a scientist? (Select all that apply)
What makes someone a good scientist? (Select all that apply)
You have to work in a laboratory to be a scientist.
Scientists always work alone and never share their ideas.
Observing means looking carefully and noticing details.
A prediction is made after you do your experiment.
In the story, Mai's flower in the sun opened after two hours.
Being curious means you don't want to learn new things.
A _____ is a person who studies the world and asks questions.
The five steps of the scientific method are: observe, question, predict, test, and _____.
When you want to know more about something, you ask a _____.
Mai lives with her grandmother in _____.
A special room where scientists do experiments is called a _____.
Being _____ means you want to learn and discover new things.
Match each vocabulary word with its correct definition:

definitions: To look at something carefully and notice details
A guess about what you think will happen
A test to find out if your prediction is right
Wanting to learn about new things

correctAnswer.Observe: To look at something carefully and notice details
correctAnswer.Predict: A guess about what you think will happen
correctAnswer.Experiment: A test to find out if your prediction is right
correctAnswer.Curious: Wanting to learn about new things

questions.text: Match each vocabulary word with its correct definition:

definitions: Something you ask when you want to know more
To try something out to see what happens
To decide what you learned from your experiment

correctAnswer.Question: Something you ask when you want to know more
correctAnswer.Test: To try something out to see what happens
correctAnswer.Conclude: To decide what you learned from your experiment

questions.text: When Mai first observed the flowers, what did she notice?
options: All flowers were the same color
Some flowers were open and some were closed
Some flowers had bees on them
questions.correctAnswer: Some flowers were open and some were closed

questions.text: What question did Mai ask after observing the flowers?
options: Do flowers open in the sun and close in the shade?
questions.correctAnswer: Do flowers open in the sun and close in the shade?

questions.text: How long did Mai wait to check her experiment?
How did Mai feel at the end of the story?
According to the lesson, where can you be a scientist? (Select all that apply)
Mai's grandmother told her to stop asking questions.
Scientists use experiments to test their predictions.
According to the lesson, you need special equipment to be a scientist.
After Mai did her experiment, she made a _____ about what she learned.
Mai's closed flowers were in the _____ under the mango tree.
What is a conclusion in the scientific method?
options: What you learned from your experiment
questions.correctAnswer: What you learned from your experiment

questions.text: After her experiment, what other questions did Mai want to explore?
options: Why rain falls and why ants walk in lines
questions.correctAnswer: Why rain falls and why ants walk in lines
